channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Hormuz Ship Insurance Demand Drops
item.description: London marine insurers have been seeing fewer inquiries for journeys to transit the Strait of Hormuz and some said the cost of cover had risen.

item.title: USA EIA Reveals Latest Oil Price Forecast
item.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration revealed its latest oil price forecast for 2026 and 2027 in its July short term energy outlook.

item.title: AEP Texas Secures $3.26B DOE Loan
item.description: The new loan is on top of a $1.6-billion loan guarantee that AEP earlier secured from the Trump administration's Energy Dominance Financing Program.

item.title: Finder Energy secures FDP approval for Kuda Tasi and Jahal project
item.description: Finder Energy has received approval for its FDP covering the KTJ offshore oil project from Timor-Leste’s petroleum regulator, the ANP.

item.title: USA Crude Oil Stocks Rise Week on Week
item.description: Crude oil stocks, not including the SPR, stood at 411.4 million barrels on July 3, the EIA's latest weekly petroleum status report showed.

item.title: NATO Projects Unity as Leaders Rally Behind Ukraine
item.description: It is fair to say that the final day of the NATO summit in Ankara on July 8 got off to a tense start. Overnight, the United States had resumed strikes on Iran after an uneasy cease-fire. The entire conflict had shaken…

item.title: Indonesia Receives First Russian Crude Shipment Under April Supply Deal
item.description: Indonesia has recently welcomed the first cargo of Russian crude oil under a deal Southeast Asia’s biggest economy struck with Moscow in April. About 770,000 barrels of Russian crude oil was delivered to Indonesia’s…

item.title: Hormuz Tanker Traffic Stops
item.description: Jorge Leon, Head of Geopolitical Analysis at Rystad, noted that tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz had 'essentially stopped'.

item.title: Shippers Cool on Green Fuels as Costs Stay Too High
item.description: The shipping industry is being blamed by climate activists for emitting a solid portion of the world’s atmospheric carbon dioxide. Under pressure, the industry has been trying to find an alternative to the traditional…

item.title: Oil falls as markets weigh US strikes on Iran and Hormuz risks
item.description: Oil prices declined on 9 July, Thursday, as traders evaluated the effects of new US military actions against Iran, raising questions about future oil flows and negotiations to resolve the ongoing conflict.

item.title: BP Weighs North Sea Exit Under New CEO
item.description: BP has started to simplify its portfolio and cut costs, and will make fewer but better choices in which projects to invest, chief executive Meg O’Neill said on Thursday. “We are taking concrete action to grow long-term…

item.title: ExxonMobil and partners commit $1bn to launch Usan Infill Project
item.description: ExxonMobil, alongside its partners, is set to invest $1bn for the on-block operations in the Usan Infill Project within Oil Mining Lease 138 (OML 138) offshore Nigeria.

item.title: High Oil Prices Are Fueling a New Heavy Crude Rush in Canada
item.description: Who says price isn't a rationing mechanism? Rather than sink billions into long-term oil sands projects, Alberta energy companies are increasingly chasing the Clearwater formation, a conventional heavy oil field that…

item.title: EU Eyes 2040 Electrification Target to Slash Oil and Gas Dependence
item.description: The European Commission is set to unveil next week an electrification target for 2040 in a bid to reduce the need for fossil fuels and strengthen the renewable energy sector in the EU, according to a draft proposal seen…

item.title: Ukraine Hits Two Russian Fuel Tankers as Moscow's Fuel Crisis Deepens
item.description: Ukraine hit two fuel tankers overnight offshore Russia’s southern coast in an expanding campaign to deepen the fuel crisis in Russia. The two tankers were hit in Taganrog Bay, the northeastern arm of the Sea of Azov in…

item.title: Marubeni sells 12.5% Big Foot stake to Modiin Energy
item.description: Japanese conglomerate Marubeni has divested its 12.5% working interest in the Big Foot oil and gas development located in the Gulf of America to Modiin Energy and Eventide Partners.

item.title: US Strikes Iran for 2nd Straight Day
item.description: U.S. Central Command said its forces completed another round of strikes 'to further degrade' Tehran's ability to attack commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: TotalEnergies Ships First LNG Cargo from Mexican Pacific Coast to Asia
item.description: 'TotalEnergies will be the sole offtaker of LNG during the ramp-up phase'.

item.title: Pakistan Rushes to Buy Emergency LNG After Qatar Cargo Is Canceled
item.description: Pakistan is forced to turn to the spot LNG market once again after the re-escalation in the Strait of Hormuz has canceled a cargo from Qatar. State-controlled Pakistan LNG has just issued a tender to buy an LNG cargo…

item.title: ADNOC Scores 15-Year Deal to Supply 1 MMtpa of LNG to INPEX
item.description: Nearly 23 percent of Ruwais LNG's 9.6 MMtpa capacity has been offtaken by Japanese customers, ADNOC said.
